Manage Consent

To provide the best experiences, we use technologies like cookies to store and/or access device information. Consenting to these technologies will allow us to process data such as browsing behavior or unique IDs on this site. Not consenting or withdrawing consent, may adversely affect certain features and functions.

FunctionalFunctional
Always active

The technical storage or access is strictly necessary for the legitimate purpose of enabling the use of a specific service explicitly requested by the subscriber or user, or for the sole purpose of carrying out the transmission of a communication over an electronic communications network.

PreferencesPreferences

The technical storage or access is necessary for the legitimate purpose of storing preferences that are not requested by the subscriber or user.

StatisticsStatistics

The technical storage or access that is used exclusively for statistical purposes.The technical storage or access that is used exclusively for anonymous statistical purposes. Without a subpoena, voluntary compliance on the part of your Internet Service Provider, or additional records from a third party, information stored or retrieved for this purpose alone cannot usually be used to identify you.

MarketingMarketing

The technical storage or access is required to create user profiles to send advertising, or to track the user on a website or across several websites for similar marketing purposes.

# 2025 Annual Meeting

- [Home](https://www.seiu509.org/)
- 2025 Annual Meeting

### JOIN US!

**2025 SEIU 509 Annual Meeting**

**Saturday, October 18** **9 AM to 4 PM**

**Hilton Boston Park Plaza**

**50 Park Plaza, Boston, MA 02116**

_\*Registration and breakfast begin at 8:30 AM_

[Click here to RSVP!](https://act.seiu.org/a/2025-annual-meeting)

[haga clic aquí para registrar](https://act.seiu.org/a/2025-annual-meeting-esp)

**On Saturday, October 18, join your fellow SEIU 509 union members at our Annual Meeting/Nominating Convention in Boston!**

The Annual Meeting is an important time for us to look back on our victories and plan to continue building worker power in the coming year! Members will also have the opportunity to submit nominations or run for a seat on one of our union leadership teams.

You must be a member in good standing to attend the Annual Meeting. If you have any questions, please email [governance@seiu509.org](mailto:governance@seiu509.org)

## accessibility & logistics​

**Hybrid meeting:** The annual meeting will be both in-person and virtual.

**Interpretation:** ASL & Spanish interpretation available for both in-person and virtual attendees.

**Childcare:** Childcare will be available at the meeting.

**Parking:** Please park at Motor Mart Garage, 201 Stuart Street, Boston, MA 02116. Parking will be covered by the union. A voucher will be available at the registration desk for attendees.

The annual meeting is a fragrance-free space for the safety and comfort of all attendees. Please do not wear or use any perfume, cologne, scented lotion, hair spray, aftershave, scented deodorant, anything aerosol (scented or unscented) or air fresheners. Unscented non-aerosol products are acceptable.

### NOMINATION GUIDELINES

- In order to run for office, a candidate must be a “member in good standing” for six months prior to the Annual Meeting.
- Nominations for all offices shall be submitted from the floor of the Annual Meeting. All nominations must be seconded.
- Nominations and seconds can be made by both in person **and** virtual member attendees. A member does not need to be present in order to be nominated.
- Nominations must be handed in writing to the Recording Secretary.
- Nominations and seconds for chapter offices shall come from the chapter and must be by members in good standing of that chapter.
- Nominations and seconds for local wide offices can come from any present member in good standing.
- All members nominated will be notified in writing that they were nominated for local wide or chapter office. If a member is nominated for more than one position, that member will be asked to choose the seat they prefer. The nomination for the higher ranking office will stand if the member does not respond.

### rules for campaigning at the annual meeting

- Candidate tables outside of the ballroom will be available on a first come first served basis
- Candidate literature is **not** allowed in the ballroom
- Candidate t-shirts and buttons are allowed in the ballroom
- Candidate banners/backgrounds/screen savers are **not** allowed for Zoom participants
- Please see the [election guide](https://www.seiu509.org/documents/election-guide-july-2022-english/) for more rules.

### candidate statements & important dates

**Candidates for contested offices may submit a statement with the following provisions:**

**DEADLINE: Candidate statements are due by noon EST on Monday, October 27.** **Statements are to be submitted to [governance@seiu509.org](mailto:governance@seiu509.org)**

**509 President:** 400 word limit and photo

**509 Treasurer, 509 Recording Secretary, and Chapter Presidents:** 300 word limit and photo

**Any other contested offices:** 200 word limit and no photo

Candidates’ statements must be transmitted to [governance@seiu509.org](mailto:governance@seiu509.org)

The candidate must submit the copy in corrected, proofread form and the Union will **not** make any changes, including the correction misspelling, grammar or typing errors.

If the statement exceeds the length specified above, the part which exceeds the limit will be cut off even if the statement terminates mid sentence.

Do **not** include a header or title.

If the statement is **not** submitted by the deadline, it will **not** be distributed or added to the website.

If you wish for your statement to be in any other language, you must email that translation at the same time. We do **not** provide translation of statements.

Candidate statements will be posted on the SEIU Local 509 website and will be distributed with the ballots.

**The following dates pertain to the ballots:**

Ballots Mail on Monday, November 10.

Ballots must be returned to the election authority on or before Wednesday, December 3.

Ballots Counted on Thursday, December 4 at 509 Headquarters.

Candidates sworn in on Tuesday, December 9 at 509 Headquarters.

All the officers of the local union elected in conformity with applicable statutes shall by virtue of such election be considered to be eligible delegates and alternates to any International Convention, Intermediate Body or Conference Convention.
